extra_model_fields.expected_outcome_after_revision: artifact preview becomes parseable; grader/verifier can confirm cell-level formulas and provenance, resulting in removal of the openpyxl_unavailable qualification and full semantic confidence.
extra_model_fields.notes: Keep changes narrowly scoped to workbook content and builder assertions to minimize regression risk. Run current tests after builder change to ensure output file path and sheet ordering remain unchanged.
verification_commands_suggestions: Use openpyxl to inspect specific cells and assert cell.value is a formula (begins with '=') for expected ranges.
Add a CI script step that runs a small Python checker to assert final_decision_table formula references raw_values and validation_checks sheet names.

planned_changes.description: Open feed_strategy_table.xlsx and confirm that every final_decision_table cell contains formulas referencing raw_values and validation_checks. Replace any hard-coded results with formula expressions preserving provenance.

Ensure all intermediate computations on the calculations sheet are implemented as spreadsheet formulas (no opaque numbers). Add adjacent cells that show the exact formula text or a provenance comment if helpful for reviewers.

Expand assumptions_and_exceptions with explicit tie-break handling (e.g., equality to threshold treated as X) and a short section on how measurement uncertainty or variability was considered in recommendations.
Add a citation column in raw_values showing the exact packet file/line reference for each input value so final_decision_table rows can be traced back to specific packet lines.

Update build_feed_strategy.mjs to include a post-build verification step that uses openpyxl (or equivalent) to programmatically assert: presence of required sheets, that specified ranges contain formulas (not constants), and that final_decision_table formula references include expected sheet names. Fail the build if checks do not pass.
Rerun the artifact build and deterministic preview in an environment with openpyxl available, capture parsed preview output, and attach it to the attempt trace (so grader/verifier can parse cells).
Update final_message.txt to include a short verification checklist and confirmation of the post-build assertions (sheets present, formulas present, assumptions updated).

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address verifier/grader qualification: enable authoritative in-sheet verification and explicitly document tie-breaks and uncertainty propagation so the workbook is fully auditable at cell level and passes automated deterministic checks that inspect formulas.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Address verifier/grader qualification: enable authoritative in-sheet verification and explicitly document tie-breaks and uncertainty propagation so the workbook is fully auditable at cell level and passes automated deterministic checks that inspect formulas.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline produced the required feed_strategy_table.xlsx with the expected sheets (raw_values, calculations, validation_checks, assumptions_and_exceptions, final_decision_table, summary). Grader and verifier concluded the artifact meets the contract and assigned a high semantic score, but direct in-sheet verification was blocked because the grader could not parse the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable). The primary remaining risk is lack of authoritative cell-level confirmation that final_decision_table rows contain live formula references to raw_values/validation_checks, that calculations are formula-driven (no hard-coded results), and that tie-break/equality handling and uncertainty propagation are explicitly documented.
